97.reason: [The regex "^&|\\|" matches either a single '&' at the start of the string OR a single '|' anywhere due to operator precedence; it should be grouped as "^(?:&|\\|)" or "^(&|\\|)" to correctly match only standalone '&' or '|' at the beginning of a token.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The regex "^&|\\|" matches either a single '&' at the start of the string OR a single '|' anywhere due to operator precedence; it should be grouped as "^(?:&|\\|)" or "^(&|\\|)" to correctly match only standalone '&' or '|' at the beginning of a token.]

230.reason: [If no token or error matches, the lexer gets stuck in an infinite loop because 'start' does not advance, causing repeated processing of the same unmatched character. This violates the requirement to detect lexical errors like single '&' or '|', especially since the error pattern "^&|\\|" only matches at the start of the remaining string, but after skipping whitespace, an invalid symbol might not be at the start anymore due to prior partial matches or missed handling. Additionally, the error detection runs only after all constants fail, but if none match and the error pattern also doesn't match (e.g., due to context), the loop never progresses.]
